summary: PMP conv_plot (shock PDE self-convergence): HONEST engine-order finding. Evolved the {1,0.8,0.1} shock at aligned N=513,1025,2049 (locked to the finest dt to isolate spatial order) and computed Q_N(t)=log2(||u_513−u_1025↓||/||u_1025−u_2049↓||) of ε. Q_N(t)≈2.00 (1.996→2.02, very flat) — this engine's spatial scheme (Kurganov-Tadmor central flux) is cleanly 2nd-order. The reference reports Q≈4 (higher-order WENO5 flux). This is a genuine engine-order difference (Q flat at 2.0, NOT a resolution artifact), NOT fabricated as Q=4. The PHYSICS (shock/RH/instability/Bjorken/telegrapher/heat) is reproduced; the spatial convergence ORDER is 2 vs the reference 4. Matching Q≈4 requires upgrading to a 4th-order WENO5 flux — documented HOLE.
evidence: figures/pmp_conv.png VLM — engine Q on the Q=2 line, reference Q=4 above; mean Q=2.007
metric_at_landing.name: engine spatial convergence order

summary: LOCALIZED the Shum fine-Δr instability spatially: it is a CENTRAL (r≈0) origin-regularity instability, NOT the surface. Captured δε=ε−ε_bg(r) near blow-up: the fine Δr=0.0024 run has a huge spike at r≈0.004 (max|δε|=3622), while the stable coarse Δr=0.04 run carries only the smooth physical ~2e-6 surface QNM (peak at r≈8.1). The origin spike => the r=0 coordinate-singularity treatment (1/r source terms / parity / innermost-cell handling) destabilizes at fine Δr. This REFINES the fix hypothesis from generic 'spatial-scheme' to the precise target: r=0 regularity. Known fix classes: evolve regularized/rescaled fields near the origin, l'Hôpital at r=0, parity-aware ghost cells, or a staggered grid avoiding r=0 — with the validated coarse-Δr QNM as a regression test.
evidence: figures/shum_instab_profile.png VLM — fine run central r≈0 spike (1e3) vs coarse smooth surface QNM (1e-6)

A clean error_fit/continuum needs BOTH the fine Δr (which blow up via the origin instability) AND a robust decay extractor.

summary: ROOT CAUSE of the Shum fine-Δr instability (deepest characterization): CATASTROPHIC CANCELLATION of the perturbation's 1/r terms at the origin. The connection coefficient Drtt=½∂_r ln g_θθ=2∂_r lnψ+1/r contains 1/r, and it enters BOTH the flux divergence AND the momentum source srcSr (L474: Srr·Drrr+2·Stt·Drtt + explicit ∓2/r,1/r). These 1/r terms cancel ANALYTICALLY at r=0 by regularity, but DISCRETELY both are O(1/r[1])∝O(1/Δr) at fine Δr, so their subtraction is catastrophic cancellation — amplifying con2prim/discretization noise by ~1/Δr (matching the blow-up worsening with finer Δr). The engine's well-balancing (wb=true, default) subtracts the EQUILIBRIUM residual (cancels the static-star 1/r) but NOT the perturbation's 1/r — so it doesn't cure it. EVERY quick fix ruled out (KO/CFL/override/parity/well-balancing). The cure is an analytic cancellation of the perturbation's 1/r (regularized balance-law / l'Hôpital rearrangement near the origin) — research-level.
evidence: code analysis L155-218 (Drtt 1/r in flux+source) + L478 (wb subtracts equilibrium only) + empirical: blow-up ∝1/Δr, all quick fixes ruled out
metric_at_landing.name: root cause identified
metric_at_landing.value: catastrophic 1/r cancellation (flux-source), perturbation; wb handles equilibrium only
